GuardrailDirection.description:
Desired direction for a guardrail metric.

``increase`` — the metric should go up (block only if lift is negative).
``decrease`` — the metric should go down (block only if lift is positive).
``flat``     — no movement is desired; any significant change blocks.

When a guardrail metric is not listed in ``guardrail_directions`` on the
config, the legacy v1 behaviour applies: any significant movement blocks.
GuardrailDirection.title: GuardrailDirection

MetricSpec.description:
Typed specification for a single secondary or guardrail metric.

Accepts either a plain string shorthand (name only, type inferred as
``continuous``) or a full object so callers can be explicit.

Examples::

    # shorthand — converted automatically
    "revenue_per_user"

    # full spec
    {"name": "revenue_per_user", "metric_type": "continuous"}

metric_type.description: Statistical type for this metric. Defaults to 'continuous' when not specified. Set to 'proportion' for binary or rate metrics.

is_guardrail.description: True when this metric is used as a guardrail.
is_guardrail.title: Is Guardrail

MetricSpec.title: MetricSpec

MetricType.description: Statistical type of the primary metric. Drives test selection.
MetricType.title: MetricType

description: Schema version 1.2 — canonical experiment configuration for abkit. Generated from abkit_core.schemas.ExperimentConfig.

primary_metric.description: Canonical name of the main success metric.
primary_metric.title: Primary Metric

metric_type.description: Statistical type of the primary metric: 'proportion' or 'continuous'. Required for analysis runs; optional for spec-validation-only runs.

expected_allocation.description: Planned traffic split by variant. Values must sum to 1.0.
expected_allocation.title: Expected Allocation

unit_of_randomization.description: Unit assigned to variants, e.g. 'user_id' or 'session_id'.
unit_of_randomization.title: Unit Of Randomization

alpha.description: Significance threshold used for planning or reporting.
alpha.title: Alpha

secondary_metrics.description: Additional metrics of interest. Each entry may be a plain string (name only) or a full MetricSpec object.
secondary_metrics.title: Secondary Metrics

guardrail_metrics.description: Metrics used to detect harmful side effects. Each entry may be a plain string (name only) or a full MetricSpec object.
guardrail_metrics.title: Guardrail Metrics

guardrail_directions.description: Optional map of guardrail metric name → desired direction. Allowed values: 'increase', 'decrease', 'flat'. When a guardrail metric is not listed here the legacy v1 behaviour applies: any statistically significant movement triggers a hold. Example: {"revenue": "increase", "refund_rate": "decrease"}.
guardrail_directions.title: Guardrail Directions

pre_period_window_days.description: Number of days used for CUPED covariates.
pre_period_window_days.title: Pre Period Window Days

srm_alpha.description: Per-experiment SRM detection threshold. Overrides the global default of 0.01. Must be between 0.001 and 0.1.
srm_alpha.title: Srm Alpha

daily_eligible_traffic.description: Expected number of eligible units entering the experiment per day.
daily_eligible_traffic.title: Daily Eligible Traffic

eligibility_rate.description: Fraction of total traffic that is eligible. Between 0 and 1 exclusive.
eligibility_rate.title: Eligibility Rate

traffic_cap.description: Maximum fraction of eligible traffic to route to the experiment. Between 0 and 1 exclusive. Defaults to 1.0 when not set.
traffic_cap.title: Traffic Cap

ramp_up_days.description: Days before full traffic allocation is reached. Treated as a planning buffer, not a novelty-effect model.
ramp_up_days.title: Ramp Up Days

planning_buffer_pct.description: Additional fractional buffer on the planned duration. e.g. 0.2 adds 20 percent.
planning_buffer_pct.title: Planning Buffer Pct

apply_bonferroni_correction.description: When True, Bonferroni correction is applied to secondary metrics. If there are m secondary metrics, significance is tested at alpha / m rather than alpha. Has no effect when there are 0 or 1 secondary metrics. The primary metric is always tested at the declared alpha. Guardrail metrics are always tested at the declared alpha.
apply_bonferroni_correction.title: Apply Bonferroni Correction

title: ExperimentConfig
